sections.heading: Product Summary
sections.content:
Product: 1% Hydrocortisone Cream, 30g
Product Type: OTC Drug (Topical Corticosteroid)
Regulatory Category: OTC Monograph Drug – FDA Category I (21 CFR Part 347)
Primary Indication: Temporary relief of itching associated with minor skin irritations, inflammation, rashes (e.g., insect bites, poison ivy, eczema)
Distribution Channel: US Drug Store – CVS and Walgreens planogram entry
Initial Stocking Quantity: 15,000 units across both chains (split [To be confirmed])

sections.heading: Purpose of This Document
sections.content: This document provides the Demand Planning team with a structured overview of the product launch parameters, demand drivers, supply constraints, and planning assumptions for the initial market entry of 1% Hydrocortisone Cream 30g into the US drug store channel. It serves as the foundational reference for all downstream demand planning activities.

sections.heading: Key Planning Parameters
sections.content:
• Retail Partners: CVS, Walgreens
• Launch Type: Planogram entry (new shelf placement)
• Initial Stocking Units: 15,000 units total
• Unit Size: 30g tube
• Replenishment Lead Time (API): 10 weeks (EU single-source supplier)
• Finished Goods Lead Time: [To be confirmed]
• Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ): [To be confirmed]
• Shelf Life / Expiry Buffer Policy: [To be confirmed]
• Safety Stock Target: [To be confirmed]

sections.heading: Regulatory Disclaimer
sections.content: The regulatory information in this document is provided for guidance only. Please verify all regulatory requirements with your qualified regulatory affairs team before taking action.

sections.heading: Forecast Methodology
sections.content:
Given this is a new product launch with no internal sales history, the demand forecast will rely on the following inputs:
• Analogous product benchmarking (comparable OTC hydrocortisone SKUs in the drug store channel)
• Retail partner planogram slot expectations and category velocity data
• Seasonal demand curve modelling based on historical category trends
• Account team intelligence from CVS and Walgreens category managers
• Sell-in vs. sell-through distinction to avoid overstocking at launch

Forecast Horizon: 18 months rolling
Forecast Cadence: Monthly (with weekly review during launch ramp)
Forecast Owner: Demand Planning Lead
Forecast Input Contributors: Sales, Marketing, Key Account Management, Supply Chain

sections.heading: Seasonality Profile
sections.content:
This product has a pronounced seasonal demand pattern driven by outdoor activity-related skin conditions:

• Peak Season: Late spring through summer (approximately May – August)
• Primary Demand Drivers: Insect bites, poison ivy, poison oak, sunburn-associated inflammation
• Secondary Demand Drivers: Year-round eczema and minor dermatitis use cases
• Estimated Seasonal Index: [To be confirmed – benchmark against category data]
• Pre-season inventory build recommendation: Stock build to begin minimum 10–12 weeks ahead of peak (February–March) to account for EU API lead time

Seasonal Demand Curve (Indicative):
• Jan–Mar: Low / Base demand — planogram replenishment only
• Apr–May: Rising demand — early season ramp
• Jun–Aug: Peak demand — highest velocity, monitor POS weekly
• Sep–Oct: Declining — manage inventory draw-down to avoid excess
• Nov–Dec: Base / Low — standard replenishment

sections.heading: Initial Launch Demand Assumptions
sections.content:
• Total Initial Stocking Order: 15,000 units
• Assumed Retailer Split: [To be confirmed – CVS vs. Walgreens allocation]
• Assumed Planogram Facings per Store: [To be confirmed]
• Estimated Store Count (CVS): [To be confirmed]
• Estimated Store Count (Walgreens): [To be confirmed]
• Anticipated Sell-Through Velocity (Weekly per Store): [To be confirmed – to be modelled against category benchmarks]
• First Replenishment Order Trigger Point: [To be confirmed – based on agreed safety stock levels and lead time]

sections.heading: Forecast Risk Factors
sections.content:
• Upside Risk: Stronger-than-expected summer season (heat waves, higher outdoor activity) could accelerate sell-through beyond base forecast
• Downside Risk: Planogram reset delays or de-listing by either retail partner could reduce initial volume
• New Product Uncertainty: No internal demand history; forecast confidence interval is wide in months 1–3
• Competitive Activity: Pricing or promotional activity from incumbent OTC hydrocortisone brands (e.g., Cortaid, store-brand generics) may suppress velocity

sections.heading: Regulatory Disclaimer
sections.content: The regulatory information in this document is provided for guidance only. Please verify all regulatory requirements with your qualified regulatory affairs team before taking action.

sections.heading: Supply Chain Overview
sections.content:
Product: 1% Hydrocortisone Cream 30g
API (Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient): Hydrocortisone 1%
API Supplier: Single EU-based supplier (name [To be confirmed])
API Lead Time: 10 weeks from purchase order to receipt
API Supply Classification: SINGLE SOURCE – HIGH RISK
Finished Goods Manufacturer: [To be confirmed]
Finished Goods Lead Time: [To be confirmed]
Packaging Supplier: [To be confirmed]

sections.heading: Single-Source API Risk
sections.content:
RISK LEVEL: HIGH

The API (hydrocortisone) is sourced exclusively from one EU supplier. This represents the most significant supply risk for this product. Key risk dimensions include:

• Geopolitical / Trade Risk: EU-to-US trade disruptions, tariffs, or export restrictions could delay or block API shipments
• Supplier Capacity Risk: Inability of the single supplier to scale output during peak demand season without advance planning
• Quality / Regulatory Risk: A supplier audit failure, FDA import alert, or batch rejection would halt supply with no alternative source available
• Lead Time Exposure: 10-week API lead time means demand signals must be acted on approximately 3–4 months before finished goods are needed on shelf
• Force Majeure / Operational Disruption: Supplier facility shutdowns (fire, flood, labour action, utility failure) have no backup option

Mitigation Recommendations:
• Initiate qualification of a secondary API supplier as a medium-term priority [Timeline: To be confirmed]
• Negotiate strategic API buffer stock held at supplier or at a bonded warehouse in the US
• Define a minimum API safety stock level to cover at least one full production run plus 4 weeks of peak demand
• Establish a supplier performance scorecard with monthly review cadence

sections.heading: Lead Time Analysis and Planning Implications
sections.content:
API Lead Time: 10 weeks
Estimated Finished Goods Manufacturing Time: [To be confirmed]
Estimated Total Supply Lead Time (API to Shelf): [To be confirmed – estimated 14–18 weeks pending FG lead time confirmation]

Planning Implications:
• Demand Planning must issue a forward demand signal to procurement a minimum of 10–12 weeks before required delivery of API
• For peak season (June–August), API purchase orders must be placed no later than February–March
• Any demand upside identified after the API PO freeze window cannot be fulfilled within the same season
• A rolling 26-week demand forecast should be shared with procurement to enable forward API purchasing decisions

sections.heading: Seasonal Supply Build Plan (Indicative)
sections.content:
• October–November: Confirm peak season demand forecast; issue API purchase order for peak season volume
• December–January: API in production / transit
• February–March: API receipt; commence finished goods manufacturing
• April: Finished goods available; pre-position inventory at DC/3PL ahead of peak
• May–August: Peak season — monitor POS weekly; trigger replenishment orders with extended lead time awareness
• September: Begin inventory draw-down; assess excess/obsolescence risk

sections.heading: Inventory Risk – Obsolescence and Expiry
sections.content:
• OTC drug products are subject to expiry dating requirements under FDA regulations
• Over-stocking ahead of peak season to mitigate supply risk must be balanced against expiry date risk
• Shelf life of finished product: [To be confirmed]
• Recommended minimum remaining shelf life for retailer acceptance (CVS, Walgreens): [To be confirmed – typically 12+ months]
• Obsolescence risk is elevated in Year 1 due to forecast uncertainty on a new product launch
• Demand Planning should model inventory scenarios at P50, P75, and P90 demand levels before committing to peak season build quantities

sections.heading: Regulatory Disclaimer
sections.content: The regulatory information in this document is provided for guidance only. Please verify all regulatory requirements with your qualified regulatory affairs team before taking action.

title: Inventory Policy & Safety Stock Recommendation – 1% Hydrocortisone Cream 30g (OTC)

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.content: This document defines the recommended inventory policy and safety stock parameters for the 1% Hydrocortisone Cream 30g OTC product to ensure continuity of supply to CVS and Walgreens while managing the risks associated with single-source API supply and seasonal demand variability.

sections.heading: Safety Stock Rationale
sections.content:
Given the following risk factors, a higher-than-standard safety stock level is warranted for this product:

• Single-source EU API supplier with a 10-week lead time
• Pronounced seasonal demand spike (summer peak) creating compressed replenishment windows
• New product launch with no historical demand data (high forecast error expected in Year 1)
• Retail channel (CVS, Walgreens) with planogram compliance requirements and potential service level penalties for out-of-stocks

sections.heading: Recommended Safety Stock Calculation Inputs
sections.content:
The following inputs are required to calculate a statistically robust safety stock level. Where not yet available, placeholders are noted:

• Average Weekly Demand (AWD): [To be confirmed – to be modelled from launch benchmarks]
• Demand Standard Deviation (σD): [To be confirmed – to be updated after 8–12 weeks of actual POS data]
• API Replenishment Lead Time: 10 weeks
• Finished Goods Manufacturing Lead Time: [To be confirmed]
• Total Lead Time (L): [To be confirmed]
• Target Service Level: [To be confirmed – recommend 95%+ given retail channel requirements]
• Safety Stock Formula: SS = Z × σD × √L (where Z = service level Z-score)

Note: Safety stock calculation should be reviewed and updated quarterly in Year 1 as actual demand data becomes available.

sections.heading: Interim Safety Stock Guidance (Pre-Launch)
sections.content:
Pending confirmed demand data, the following interim safety stock approach is recommended:

• Minimum Safety Stock: Equivalent to 10 weeks of average demand (to cover one full API replenishment cycle)
• Peak Season Additional Buffer: Additional 4–6 weeks of peak demand volume to be held as seasonal safety stock during May–August
• DC/3PL Buffer Location: [To be confirmed]
• Retailer DC Inventory Target: [To be confirmed with CVS and Walgreens supply chain teams]

sections.heading: Reorder Point Policy
sections.content:
• Reorder Point (ROP) = Average Demand during Lead Time + Safety Stock
• Given 10-week API lead time, reorder signals for API must be generated when on-hand + on-order inventory drops to the calculated ROP
• Demand Planning is responsible for generating weekly inventory position reports and flagging reorder triggers to Procurement
• During peak season (May–August), reorder point review should shift to weekly cadence

sections.heading: Inventory Targets Summary
sections.content:
• Initial Stocking Quantity: 15,000 units (sell-in to CVS and Walgreens)
• Safety Stock Target (Ongoing): [To be confirmed – pending demand modelling]
• Peak Season Inventory Build Target: [To be confirmed]
• Weeks of Supply Target (Non-Peak): [To be confirmed – recommend 12–14 weeks given lead time]
• Weeks of Supply Target (Peak): [To be confirmed – recommend 16–20 weeks given demand volatility]
• Excess & Obsolescence Review Frequency: Quarterly

sections.heading: Regulatory Disclaimer
sections.content: The regulatory information in this document is provided for guidance only. Please verify all regulatory requirements with your qualified regulatory affairs team before taking action.

title: Launch Readiness Checklist – Demand Planning – 1% Hydrocortisone Cream 30g (OTC)

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.content: This checklist ensures the Demand Planning team has completed all required activities prior to the initial stocking of 1% Hydrocortisone Cream 30g at CVS and Walgreens. It is intended to be reviewed in the S&OP/launch readiness meeting.

sections.heading: Pre-Launch Checklist – Demand Planning
sections.content:
FORECASTING & PLANNING
• ☐ Initial 18-month demand forecast completed and reviewed with Sales and Marketing
• ☐ Seasonal demand curve modelled and agreed (summer peak acknowledged)
• ☐ Forecast submitted to S&OP process and approved
• ☐ CVS and Walgreens initial stocking quantities confirmed (15,000 units total – split allocation [To be confirmed])
• ☐ Sell-in vs. sell-through tracking mechanism established
• ☐ POS data feed agreed with both retail partners for ongoing demand sensing

SUPPLY & PROCUREMENT
• ☐ API purchase order placed with EU supplier for initial stocking volume
• ☐ API safety stock policy agreed with Procurement
• ☐ Peak season API pre-buy plan approved (accounting for 10-week lead time)
• ☐ Finished goods manufacturing schedule confirmed
• ☐ 3PL / DC receiving and storage capacity confirmed for 15,000-unit initial stock
• ☐ Secondary API supplier qualification initiated (risk mitigation)

RETAIL EXECUTION
• ☐ Planogram entry dates confirmed with CVS and Walgreens
• ☐ Store count confirmed for each retail partner
• ☐ Initial store-level allocation plan prepared
• ☐ Retailer service level requirements confirmed (fill rate, lead time expectations)
• ☐ EDI / order management integration confirmed with both retail partners

INVENTORY & RISK
• ☐ Safety stock targets set and documented
• ☐ Reorder point policy established
• ☐ Excess & obsolescence risk reviewed and approved by Finance
• ☐ Expiry date management policy confirmed with QA and retail partners
• ☐ Supply risk escalation protocol agreed for single-source API disruption scenarios

REGULATORY & COMPLIANCE
• ☐ OTC drug labelling confirmed compliant with FDA monograph requirements (21 CFR Part 347)
• ☐ NDC number assigned and registered
• ☐ Lot traceability and serialisation requirements confirmed
• ☐ Import documentation for EU API shipments reviewed by Regulatory Affairs

sections.heading: Open Items / Actions
sections.content:
• CVS vs. Walgreens unit allocation split: [To be confirmed – Owner: Key Account Management]
• Finished goods lead time: [To be confirmed – Owner: Supply Chain]
• Retailer DC service level requirements: [To be confirmed – Owner: Sales / Customer Supply Chain]
• Secondary API supplier qualification timeline: [To be confirmed – Owner: Procurement]
• POS data feed setup with retail partners: [To be confirmed – Owner: Customer Insights / IT]

sections.content: The regulatory information in this document is provided for guidance only. Please verify all regulatory requirements with your qualified regulatory affairs team before taking action.

title: S&OP Input Brief – 1% Hydrocortisone Cream 30g (OTC) – New Product Launch

sections.heading: Document Purpose
sections.content: This brief provides the Sales & Operations Planning (S&OP) process with all required demand planning inputs for the new launch of 1% Hydrocortisone Cream 30g into the US drug store channel (CVS and Walgreens). It is intended to facilitate cross-functional alignment across Demand Planning, Supply Chain, Procurement, Sales, Finance, and Regulatory Affairs.

sections.heading: Product & Channel Summary
sections.content:
• Product: 1% Hydrocortisone Cream, 30g OTC Drug
• Channels: CVS (drug store), Walgreens (drug store)
• Launch Type: Planogram entry – new SKU
• Initial Stocking Volume: 15,000 units
• Retailer Allocation Split: [To be confirmed]
• Anticipated Planogram Entry Date: [To be confirmed]

sections.heading: Demand Signal
sections.content:
• 18-Month Baseline Forecast: [To be confirmed – in development]
• Year 1 Volume Estimate: [To be confirmed]
• Seasonal Pattern: Summer peak (June–August) driven by insect bite and poison ivy season
• Forecast Confidence: LOW in months 1–3 (new product, no history); increases post-launch as POS data becomes available
• Key Demand Assumptions:
  - Planogram compliance achieved at both CVS and Walgreens
  - No major competitive promotional disruption
  - Normal summer weather patterns (not adjusted for extreme heat scenarios)
  - Sell-through velocity benchmarked against comparable OTC hydrocortisone SKUs

sections.heading: Supply Constraints for S&OP Awareness
sections.content:
• API Lead Time: 10 weeks (EU single-source supplier) — this is the primary supply constraint
• Total Supply Lead Time to Shelf: [To be confirmed]
• Peak Season Supply Risk: High — API orders for summer peak must be placed by February/March at the latest
• Single-Source API Risk: No backup supplier currently qualified — escalation required if EU supplier disruption occurs
• Recommended S&OP Action: Approve peak season API pre-buy in [Month TBC] S&OP cycle to ensure summer availability

sections.heading: Financial Planning Inputs
sections.content:
• Initial Stocking Revenue (Sell-In): [To be confirmed – pending unit pricing]
• Forecast Revenue (Year 1): [To be confirmed]
• Inventory Investment (Safety Stock + Peak Build): [To be confirmed]
• Excess & Obsolescence Reserve Recommendation: [To be confirmed – Finance to model based on P50/P90 demand scenarios]
• COGS Impact of Single-Source API: [To be confirmed – Procurement to provide API unit cost]

sections.heading: Key Decisions Required from S&OP
sections.content:
1. Approve peak season API pre-buy quantity and timing
2. Confirm safety stock investment level for Year 1 launch period
3. Align on CVS vs. Walgreens initial stocking unit split
4. Approve secondary API supplier qualification as a funded project
5. Set target customer service level (fill rate) for CVS and Walgreens
6. Confirm escalation authority and protocol for single-source API supply disruption
